LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MARCH-27-2020 HEARING RE: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, OFF CALENDAR. IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100106267) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CAMPOS, CARLOS JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-14-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GARY CALLIS' MOTION TO STRIKE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GARY CALLIS' "MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S ENTIRE COMPLAINT" IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 435.5. THAT STATUTE REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE," WHICH COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES HAS NOT OCCURRED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND CALLIS' RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY SEPTEMBER 4, 2020. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR#5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GARY CALLIS' MOTION TO STRIKE plaintiff Carlos Campos' entire complaint is granted. On December 31, 2017, Callis and Campos had a traffic accident in San Francisco. Callis sued Campos in this court in Case No. CGC-18-569401. Campos answered without filing a cross-complaint. Callis dismissed 569401 in September 2019. In November 2019, Campos sued Callis in Case No. CGC-19-580949 over the same December 31, 2017 traffic accident. (Judicial notice of all the above is taken as requested.) Campos' November 2019 suit was barred by Code of Civil Procedure 426.30(a), which provides: "if a party against whom a complaint has been filed and served fails to allege in a cross-complaint any related cause of action which (at the time of serving his answer to the complaint) he has against the plaintiff, such party may not thereafter in any other action assert against the plaintiff the related cause of action not pleaded."

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-11-2020 Continued To Nov-25-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-14-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant Gary Callis' Motion To Strike.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Gary Callis' "motion to strike plaintiff's entire complaint" is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 435.5. That statute requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone," which counsel's declaration indicates has not occurred. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Callis' response to the complaint is now due by September 4, 2020.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/RBU)
